themes.title: Trump’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: The attempted assassination of Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner has become a dominant transnational narrative, with right-wing media across multiple countries framing it as an attack on democracy and a symbol of rising political violence. Coverage emphasizes security failures, Trump’s defiance, and broader implications for political polarization.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Intense focus on security lapses, Trump’s reaction, and the suspect’s anti-Trump manifesto. Articles highlight Trump’s calls for unity and criticism of media coverage.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni’s solidarity with Trump, condemnation of political violence, and framing of the incident as part of a broader pattern of left-wing extremism.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Coverage emphasizes the attack as evidence of global political instability and aligns with Trump’s narrative of victimhood.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage and others use the incident to criticize U.S. security and media, while drawing parallels to domestic political tensions.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Abascal condemns the attack as political violence, linking it to broader critiques of left-wing radicalism and media bias.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD frames the incident as part of a global trend of political violence, using it to critique U.S. and European security policies.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish leaders condemn the attack, emphasizing solidarity with Trump and framing it as an assault on democratic values.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are experiencing a surge in polling and electoral success, with coverage highlighting their growing influence in national and regional politics. The narrative emphasizes their role in coalition governments, policy shifts, and rising public support amid economic and migration crises.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads national polls in Germany, with record support and growing influence in state and local elections. Coverage focuses on internal conflicts, policy proposals, and opposition to immigration.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Bardella and Le Pen’s party gains traction ahead of the 2027 presidential election, with coverage highlighting their anti-immigration stance and rising popularity among business leaders.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s coalition deals with PP in Aragón and Extremadura, and its push for 'national priority' policies, dominate coverage. Articles highlight internal disputes and electoral strategies.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Sweden Democrats’ growing influence in Swedish politics, including their role in shaping immigration and education policies, is a key focus of coverage.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: Norway’s Progress Party gains ground in local elections, with coverage emphasizing its anti-immigration and economic liberalization policies.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK’s rising support in UK polls, particularly in local elections, and its anti-immigration platform, are central to coverage.

themes.title: PP-Vox coalitions spark constitutional debates
themes.blurb: The formation of coalition governments between Spain’s Partido Popular (PP) and Vox in regions like Aragón, Extremadura, and Castilla y León has ignited transnational debates over constitutional legality, immigration policies, and the rise of far-right influence in mainstream politics. Coverage highlights tensions between national and regional governments, as well as legal challenges to these coalitions.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s 'national priority' policy, which prioritizes Spaniards for social aid and housing, dominates coverage. Articles highlight its implementation in regional governments and legal challenges.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Partido Popular
countries.note: PP’s coalition deals with Vox in Aragón and Extremadura, and its shifting stance on immigration and social policies, are central to coverage. Articles emphasize internal divisions and electoral strategies.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: French far-right coverage draws parallels between Vox’s policies and RN’s own anti-immigration platform, framing the coalitions as part of a broader European trend.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing media critiques Vox’s policies, particularly their impact on EU cohesion and constitutional legality, while drawing comparisons to Italy’s own far-right governance.

themes.title: Orbán’s resignation reshapes Hungarian politics
themes.blurb: Viktor Orbán’s decision to step down as Hungary’s prime minister and his party’s historic election defeat have triggered a seismic shift in Hungarian politics. Coverage focuses on the rise of Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party, the reorganization of Fidesz, and the implications for Hungary’s relationship with the EU and its frozen funds.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Fidesz’s internal reorganization, Orbán’s resignation, and the rise of the Tisza Party dominate coverage. Articles highlight the party’s shift to opposition and efforts to regain public trust.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Polish media frames Orbán’s resignation as a cautionary tale for PiS, emphasizing the risks of authoritarian overreach and the importance of EU relations.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD coverage draws parallels between Orbán’s downfall and its own political struggles, while critiquing the EU’s role in Hungary’s political transition.

countries.name: 🇪🇺 EU Relations
countries.note: EU media focuses on Hungary’s frozen funds, the Tisza Party’s negotiations with Brussels, and the potential for Hungary to realign with EU priorities.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right media across multiple countries are amplifying narratives that link inflammatory rhetoric to acts of political violence. Coverage highlights incidents of violence against politicians, journalists, and marginalized groups, while also critiquing the role of far-right discourse in escalating tensions.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian media highlights Salvini’s rhetoric and its impact on political violence, including attacks on left-wing activists and journalists.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s inflammatory language, particularly its attacks on political opponents and migrants, is linked to rising political violence and protests against its rallies.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK’s anti-immigration rhetoric and its ties to far-right groups are scrutinized for fueling political violence and protests.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump’s rhetoric and its role in inspiring political violence, including the WHCD shooting, are central to coverage. Articles highlight the normalization of violent discourse in U.S. politics.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: French media critiques RN’s rhetoric, particularly its anti-immigration stance, for contributing to political polarization and violence.
